titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the George Frederick Gundelfinger Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by Staff of Manuscripts and Archives

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

conventiondeclaration.abbr: Finding aid created in accordance with Manuscripts and Archives Processing Manual

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday June 3, 2023 at 10:33

did.abstract: The papers consist of correspondence, publications of The New Fraternity, and other books, pamphlets, and unpublished writings of George Frederick Gundelfinger who made a lifetime crusade of sending his publications and moral admonitions to Yale University. Yale's suit against him in 1939 for sending obscenity through the mail is documented by trial transcripts. Also in the papers are photographs, newspaper clippings, printed matter, and books on mathematics. There is also an edited diary describing a family tour of Europe in 1908.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of George Gundelfinger, 1973. Transfer from Hall of Graduate Studies, 2004. Gift of Elizabeth Gundelfinger, 2016.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: George Frederick Gundelfinger Papers (MS 693). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The papers of George Frederick Gundelfinger contain correspondence, newspaper clippings, publications of The New Fraternity, and other books, pamphlets, and unpublished writings by Gundelfinger. The transcripts of his trial are also included, as are diplomas, sheet music for compositions by Gundelfinger, paintings by him, photographs, printing plates and layouts for publications, and writings by others, mostly on mathematics. The papers are completely unprocessed and disorganized, and contain numerous duplicates of many of the publications. There is also an addition which contains an edited diary describing a family tour of Europe in 1908.
The following pages contain a box list of the collection and a more detailed list of printed items in the papers; this list is not comprehensive.
These papers were donated to Yale University in 1973 by George Frederick Gundelfinger. Further correspondence and other papers can be found in the Yale Archives, RU 50.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: George Frederick Gundelfinger, a teacher of mathematics and a writer, was born in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania, in 1884. He earned a B.S. from Sheffield Scientific School, Yale University in 1906 and a Ph.D. in math in 1909. He taught math at Yale from 1909 to 1913, and he also taught at the University of Pittsburgh and at Carnegie Institute of Technology. He wrote and printed the Interquadrangular, an independent Yale paper, and founded the brotherhood, "The New Fraternity." Upon retiring from teaching, Gundelfinger began a lifetime of moral crusading by sending his publications and admonitions to the students of Yale University.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

did.unittitle: Letters to officials, entitled "Back o' Bars"
Pamphlets addressed to Judge Wallace S. Courley
Three scrapbooks of pamphlets and leaflets with printing costs
Collage of sketches relating to The New Fraternity
List of Printed Items in the George Frederick Gundelfinger Papers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: (*) Number in parentheses relates to bibliography printed in The Storm of Protest, 1944.

did.unittitle: Yale's New Fraternity versus Roman Catholic Fascism (63)*
Gundelfinger's reply to Gorokhoff's Question, 4 pp., 8.5 x 5.5"
The Interquadrangular - Yale's Ultimate Social System, 14 pp., 8.5 x 5.5"
The New Fraternity: Yale's Anniversary 250th: (1916 Pub.), stamped, Emerson-Conduct of Life, Novel "New Frat." (5)*
The New Fraternity: Yale's Anniversary 250th, not stamped (5)*
The New Fraternity: Yale's Anniversary -Fly Leaves, stamped in black for page 263.
The New Fraternity: Parry Profile, Harkness Tower, place in book pages 262-263
The New Fraternity, 1916, light blue cover and dust jacket (5)*
The Man of the New Fraternity, reprint of Yale News - That Fellow Gundelfinger is to be reprinted on blank side from Yale News, 12 x 16", 1 sheet
War as a Means for Abolishing War, 4 pp., 8.5 x 5.5"
James Rowland to the Dark Tower (66)*
Yale's New Fraternity versus Roman Catholic Fascism (63)*
The Decay of Bulldogism, 16 pp., 8.5 x 5.5"
The Decay of Bulldogism, 4threprint, 16 pp., 8.5 x 5.5"
Why I am Opposed to College Fraternities (24)*
Yale New Fraternity in Self-Defense, 11 pp., 8.5 x 5.5" (80)*
Yale Critic "Humorist" says Yale News of Gundelfinger, 4 pp.
An Original Interpretation of Sexual Phenomena (9)*
The Disrespect for Scholarship, The Decline of Teaching, The Failure to Educate (37)*
Inter-Q As a Column, 4 pp., 8.5 x 5.5"
A Needless (?) Introduction to Seymour's Yale (72)*
The Storm of Protest That Will Never Come (85)*
The End of an Era, 7 pp. 8.5 x 5.5"
Vaterland and Alma Mater, 17 pp., 8.5 x 5.5"
End of Era, flier, 1 p., 10 x 15.5"
Yale's New Fraternity versus Roman Catholic Fascism (63)*
I Think of You, sheet music
All the World is in Love, sheet music
When Jerry Plays the Flute, sheet music
I Love to be Loved by a Baby, sheet music
Doing Europe in 1908: Four Diaries in One , by Bertha C., Norma M., Hilda A., and George F. Gundelfinger, edited and illustrated by George F. Gundelfinger
